francis.title: The Wolf of Gubbio
francis.story: A fierce wolf terrorizes a town. Francis chooses to meet it with mercy.

challenge.prompt: How do you respond to fear with mercy?
options: Offer peace and listen to the wolf's pain
Drive the wolf away with force
Ignore it and move on
Blame the townspeople

francis.title: Sermon to the Birds
francis.story: Francis teaches that all creation is loved by God.

challenge.question: Which virtue best fits caring for all of creation?

francis.title: Rebuild My Church
francis.story: Francis hears Christ call him to rebuild the Church.

challenge.prompt: What is the best first step?
options: Start with small acts of love and service
Wait for others to do it
Seek fame for the effort
Ignore the call

carlo.title: The Digital Evangelist
carlo.story: Carlo uses technology to share Eucharistic miracles.

challenge.question: What does the Eucharist become at Mass?

carlo.title: Coding for the Kingdom
carlo.story: Carlo builds a website to teach others about the faith.

challenge.prompt: How can you use your talents for God?
options: Teach others what you've learned
Keep it to yourself
Use it only for popularity
Give up on it

carlo.title: Daily Mass
carlo.story: Carlo loves daily Mass and adoration.

challenge.question: Which virtue is strengthened by regular prayer?

joseph.title: The Dreamer
joseph.story: Joseph receives a dream from God about taking Mary as his wife despite her pregnancy.

challenge.prompt: What does Joseph do when faced with uncertainty and social pressure?
options: Trust God's message and take Mary as his wife
Quietly divorce her to avoid shame
Seek advice from others before deciding
Wait for another sign before acting

joseph.title: Provider and Protector
joseph.story: Joseph works hard to provide for Jesus and Mary, fleeing to Egypt to protect them from Herod.

challenge.question: Which virtue is most shown by Joseph's work as a carpenter to provide for his family?

joseph.title: The Silent Saint
joseph.story: Though Scripture records no words spoken by Joseph, his actions speak volumes about his character.

challenge.prompt: How can we show our virtues without needing to talk about them?
options: Let our actions speak for themselves
Tell everyone about our good deeds
Wait for others to notice before acting
Only act when we know we'll be praised

therese.title: The Little Way
therese.story: Therese discovers that holiness is found in doing small things with great love.

challenge.prompt: How can we practice love in our daily lives?
options: Do small kindnesses with great love
Wait for big opportunities to show love
Only love those who love us back
Love others to be seen as holy

therese.title: Shower of Roses
therese.story: Therese promises to send roses from heaven as a sign of her intercession.

challenge.question: What does Therese's 'little way' teach us about spiritual growth?
choices: Holiness comes from faithfulness in small things
We must perform great deeds to be holy
Spiritual growth requires isolation from others
We need to earn God's love through perfection

therese.title: Offering Up Suffering
therese.story: Therese offers her daily struggles and sufferings as gifts to God.

challenge.prompt: How can we respond to daily frustrations and inconveniences?
options: Offer them as small sacrifices of love
Complain loudly about the inconvenience
Avoid situations that might be difficult
See suffering as punishment from God

kolbe.title: The Knight of the Immaculata
kolbe.story: Kolbe founds a movement to spread devotion to Mary and combat evil through good publications.

challenge.prompt: How should we respond to evil and hatred in the world?
options: Fight evil with love and truth
Respond to hatred with more hatred
Withdraw from society to stay pure
Ignore evil and focus only on personal holiness

kolbe.title: Auschwitz Volunteer
kolbe.story: In Auschwitz, Kolbe volunteers to die in place of a stranger who has a family.

challenge.question: What ultimate act of love did Kolbe perform in Auschwitz?
choices: He died so another man could live
He shared his food with everyone
He escaped and told the world about the camp
He became the camp leader to improve conditions

kolbe.title: The Power of the Press
kolbe.story: Kolbe uses printing presses to spread religious materials and combat secularism.

challenge.prompt: How can we use modern communication tools for good?
options: Share truth and beauty to uplift others
Spread content that gets the most views
Only communicate with people who agree with us
Use technology to put others down

bernadette.title: The Vision at the Grotto
bernadette.story: Bernadette sees a beautiful lady who identifies herself as the Immaculate Conception.

challenge.prompt: How should we respond when others doubt our experiences?
options: Stay faithful to what we know is true
Change our story to please others
Doubt ourselves when others disagree
Stop sharing our experience entirely

bernadette.title: The Spring of Lourdes
bernadette.story: The lady directs Bernadette to dig in the ground, where a spring of healing water appears.

challenge.question: What does Bernadette's humble obedience teach us?
choices: God often works through the simple and humble
Only special people receive visions from God
We must earn miracles through perfection
Miracles only happen in holy places

bernadette.title: Suffering with Joy
bernadette.story: Despite her illnesses and hardships, Bernadette maintains a joyful spirit.

challenge.prompt: How can we maintain joy even when facing difficulties?
options: Find joy in loving God and others despite circumstances
Pretend everything is fine when it's not
Wait for circumstances to change before being joyful
See joy as a reward for avoiding suffering

padrepio.title: The Saint with the Wounds
padrepio.story: Padre Pio bears the wounds of Christ (stigmata) and offers suffering for the salvation of souls.

challenge.prompt: How can we view our own sufferings in light of Christ's love?
options: Offer our sufferings in union with Christ for others
See suffering as meaningless punishment
Avoid all suffering at any cost
Only accept suffering that makes us look holy

padrepio.title: The Confessor Who Knew Souls
padrepio.story: Padre Pio spends hours in the confessional, often knowing penitents' sins before they speak.

challenge.question: What virtue is most important when seeking forgiveness and healing?
choices: Trying to appear better than we are

padrepio.title: Prayer and Suffering
padrepio.story: Padre Pio says, 'Prayer is the best weapon we have; it is the key to God's heart.'

challenge.prompt: How should we respond to anxiety and worry?
options: Bring our worries to God in prayer
Worry more to show we care
Distract ourselves with entertainment
See prayer as useless when we're anxious

johnpaulii.title: The Pope Who Forgave His Assassin
johnpaulii.story: After being shot and seriously wounded, John Paul II visits his attacker in prison to offer forgiveness.

challenge.prompt: How can we respond when someone deeply hurts us?
options: Offer forgiveness while seeking justice
Seek revenge to make them pay
Hold a grudge forever
Pretend it didn't hurt us at all

johnpaulii.title: Be Not Afraid
johnpaulii.story: John Paul II's famous call to overcome fear with faith and trust in God.

challenge.question: What does John Paul II teach us about fear and faith?
choices: Trust in God's love helps us overcome fear
We should never feel afraid of anything
Fear shows we lack faith completely
We must earn courage through good deeds

johnpaulii.title: The Pilgrim Pope
johnpaulii.story: John Paul II travels more than any pope before him, bringing the gospel to the ends of the earth.

challenge.prompt: How can we share our faith with others respectfully?
options: Share our faith while respecting others' dignity
Only share with people who already believe like us
Force others to accept our beliefs
Keep our faith completely private
